title: verbascum × nothum
intro: Verbascum × nothum, also known as the mullein plant, is a hybrid of the Verbascum thapsus and Verbascum lychnitis, both native to Europe and Asia. The plant has been used for centuries for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can be attributed to its high concentration of mucilage and saponins. The health benefits of Verbascum × nothum include soothing respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as providing relief from skin irritations and wounds. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to treat various health conditions, and is also used in traditional medicine for its expectorant properties.
